mnemonics.title: Deletion-Contraction Remember
mnemonics.content: D-C REMEMBER: Delete the edge (count all colorings), Contract it (merge vertices). SUBTRACT contraction from deletion. P(G) = P(G-e) - P(G/e). Think: 'Delete Before Contract' or D-C = Delete and Count, then subtract Contracted result.

mnemonics.title: Chromatic Polynomial of Common Graphs
mnemonics.content:
TREE: k(k-1)^(n-1) - Think Tree = k then branches: (k-1) for each step.
PATH: Same as tree.
COMPLETE: k!/(k-n)! = falling factorial.
CYCLE odd: (k-1)^n + (k-1)
CYCLE even: (k-1)^n - (k-1)

acronyms.expansion: Chromatic Polynomial of graph G with k colors

acronyms.usage: Minimum colors needed, smallest k where P(G,k) > 0

visualTips.visualization: Draw the original graph, show two branches: one with edge removed, one with edge contracted (vertices merged). Use tree diagram with branches showing each recursive step.

Imagine placing n people in a row where no one sits next to someone with the same 'color' (attribute). For Kn, everyone needs unique color. Count: k choices for 1st, (k-1) for 2nd, down to (k-n+1) for nth.
For cycle: Think of wrapping around. For even cycle, you can alternate 2 colors perfectly. For odd, you need 3rd color somewhere. Use polygon visualization.

analogies.analogy: Like a birthday cake countdown - P(G,k) tells you how many 'ways' to arrange k colors on n vertices, just like n! tells you arrangements of people.
Like assigning seats at a dinner table where no married couples sit together, but strangers can. Each edge is a 'couple' that can't share a color (seat).

analogies.analization: Think of edge as a 'choice point': either you keep it separate (deletion) or you merge it (contraction). Count both and subtract the overlap.
